title: disclose report
description: Drop-in GHSA body plus a write-back audit trail.

ghsa.description: Body for POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/security-advisories. Keys match GitHub's repository-advisories REST schema so the block can be submitted as-is.

summary.description: Advisory title. Under 80 characters. Maps to GHSA `summary`.

vulnerabilities.description: Affected products. One entry per published package. Omit entirely for source-only advisories.

ecosystem.description: GHSA ecosystem enum. Lowercase. `pip` not `pypi`, `rubygems` not `RubyGems`.

vulnerable_functions.description: Exported symbols that contain the sink. Optional; fill only when the Trace names them.

severity.description: Qualitative severity. Use only when a CVSS vector cannot be derived.

cve_id.description: CVE identifier. Pass through whatever the finding carries; null when unset.

patched.description: Field names sent in the PATCH /findings/{id} body. Subset of: title, cvss_vector, cvss_v4_vector, affected, fix_version, disclosure_draft, suggested_recipients.

preserved.description: Fields left untouched because the finding already carried an analyst-set value.

suggested_recipients.description: Comma-joined file-level owners for the finding's location, each with provenance: CODEOWNERS entries (last matching pattern wins) or, absent those, recent non-bot committers from git log. Mirrors the value PATCHed to the finding. Empty string when both routes came up dry: the PATCH still sends it so a stale value from an earlier run is cleared, and `notes` says why.

references_skipped.description: Number of references skipped because the URL was already on the finding.

notes.description: Free-form prose: gaps in the source prose, ambiguity in the version range, source-only (no packages), anything the analyst should know.

error.description: Set only when the skill refused to draft (missing finding_id, insufficient prose).
